texto: I'm not saying that Beautiful People don't have the right to exist... I'm not saying they should be rounded up and taken to an island. I'm just saying that they are never, ever to be trusted because they can never know what it's like not to be Beautiful and their priority will always be being Beautiful with other Beautiful People.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Laura Bozo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I'm not saying that Beautiful People don't have the right to exist... I'm not saying they should …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, beautiful, saying pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: I'm not saying that Beautiful People don't have the right to exist... I'm not saying they should be rounded up and taken to an island. I'm just saying that they are never, ever to be trusted because they can never know what it's like not to be Beautiful and their priority will always be being Beautiful with other Beautiful People.
